mnemonics: D.C. POLO: Decentralized Control, Priority Lines Only
keyPoints: Bus arbitration resolves conflicts when multiple devices request the bus simultaneously.
Methods include Daisy-Chaining (serial priority), Polling (centralized round-robin), and Independent Requesting (parallel with priority decoder).
The arbitration method directly impacts system performance, fairness, and cost.
commonMistakes: Assuming the device physically closest to the arbiter in a daisy-chain has the highest priority (it's the device with the lowest priority number).
Confusing bus arbitration (who gets the bus) with data transfer (how data is sent on the bus).
